The Senior Manager, Events & Experiences is responsible for leading the strategy and execution of D&B’s owned event portfolio, including customer-facing programs (roundtables, advisory boards, conferences, and webinars) and high-touch internal executive experiences. This role translates enterprise event strategy into high-impact programs that deepen customer engagement, strengthen executive relationships, and support business priorities.

The role leads a small team and ensures consistent delivery of elevated, “white-glove” experiences across internal and external audiences.

lists.content:
• Lead the planning and execution of D&B’s owned event portfolio, including: customer roundtables and executive dinners, Customer Advisory Boards, Proprietary conferences and hosted events, and virtual events and webinars.

  • Translate enterprise events and experiential strategy into scalable, repeatable programs that drive engagement, pipeline, and customer insights.

  • Partner with marketing, product, and sales leadership to align owned event programs to key audiences, verticals, and business priorities.

  • Own the design and delivery of high-touch internal and executive events, including: Sales Kickoffs and CEO Circle and Executive Meetings.

  • Ensure all internal experiences reflect a premium, white-glove standard, elevating executive engagement, storytelling, and brand perception.

  • Partner with executive stakeholders, communications, and People team to align messaging, experience design, and outcomes.

  • Manage and develop a small, high-performing team, driving accountability, collaboration, and growth.

  • Lead end-to-end execution across owned and internal event programs, including: agenda and content development, experience design, vendor and production management, logistics, and budget tracking.

  • Establish and maintain high standards for execution, attendee experience, and operational rigor across all programs - and define and track success metrics.

• Bachelor's degree in marketing or a related field; Master's preferred.

  • A minimum of 5 years of related experience in event marketing, experiential programs, or event operations within a B2B of fast paced environment, with prior leadership experience.

  • Proven experience managing owned events and executive-level experiences, including both in-person and virtual formats

  •  Strong ability to design and deliver high-touch, premium experiences for senior audiences

  • Experience working directly with executive stakeholders and leadership teams, with strong stakeholder management skills.

  • Strong collaboration and influencing skills across cross-functional and global teams

  • Ability to translate strategic priorities into well-executed, high-impact programs

  • Exceptional project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple complex programs simultaneously

  • Strong business acumen and understanding of how events drive customer engagement, pipeline, and retention

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suites Skills

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

  • Willingness to travel and support events outside standard business hours as required

• Ability to sit, speak and operate telephone and/or computer for long periods of time

  • Ability to handle pressure, stressful conditions, and conflict resolution

  • Willingness to work non‑standard hours, including evenings or occasional weekends, to support global time zones

  • Ability to travel by any form of transportation; 50-60% global travel required

  • Ability to stand, walk, climb, kneel, crouch and bend over for work at office premises and office events

  • Regular attendance in the office; Hybrid role: 2–3 days in the office requirement

The Senior External Communications Manager leads the development and execution of strategic media relations and external communications initiatives that bolster the organization's reputation and market positioning. This role translates complex data and business insights into compelling narratives that resonate with media, analysts, partners, clients, and other key external audiences. This is a highly visible role that works closely with senior leaders across the organization to shape and amplify strategic communications priorities that support the business.

lists.content:
• Develop and execute external communications strategies aligned to organizational objectives and market priorities.

  • Lead media relations strategy and execution, including proactive pitching, reactive response, and cultivating relationships across AI, technology, business, financial, and industry media.

  • Guide the development of press releases, media pitches, Q&A documents, social media content, and executive talking points, ensuring quality and messaging consistency.

  • Translate complex data and proprietary business insights into clear, compelling narratives for external audiences, including media, analysts, clients, prospects, and partners.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional subject matter experts to validate data-driven findings and ensure methodological rigor in external communications.

  • Manage public relations agency relationships, directing strategy, priorities, and execution to maximize media impact and program effectiveness.

  • Define success metrics and track performance of external communications programs, leveraging media analytics and reporting to assess impact and inform strategic planning.

• Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, journalism, or a related field is required

  • 8+ years of experience in communications, public relations, journalism, or marketing in either an agency or in-house setting.

  • Background in business, financial services, data and analytics, or technology sectors preferred.

  • Demonstrated experience developing and executing strategic external communications programs, including media relations and executive visibility initiatives.

  • Strong writing, editing, and storytelling skills with the ability to translate complex data and business topics into clear, compelling narratives for diverse external audiences.

  • Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs under tight deadlines.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across functions at all levels of the organization.

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do. Be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action. Be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

• Ability to sit, speak and operate telephone and/or computer for long periods of time

  • Ability to handle pressure, stressful conditions, and conflict resolution

  • Ability to work day, evening and/or weekend hours as needed

  • Ability to stand, walk, climb, kneel, crouch and bend over for work at office premises and office events

  • Regular attendance in the office

The Senior Manager, Quote to Cash leads quote-to-cash product and process enhancements for assigned region, balancing competing priorities across product and sales teams while coordinating  closely with the Virtual Back Office (VBO) usage metering system. This role provides leadership to resources supporting the Configure, Price, and Contract Management workstreams and is accountable for resolving financial issues and discrepancies between systems.

lists.content:
• Accountable for the planning and design of assigned region’s quote-to-cash enhancements, including product design, process optimization, and automated entitlement improvements. Optimize CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) and CLM (Contract Lifecycle Management) design for international roll-out.

  • Partner with business stakeholders to translate product, pricing, and revenue requirements into scalable CPQ. Provide leadership to the CPQ and CLM teams by establishing and promoting best practices in product design. 

  • Oversee and resolve accounting alignment across order management and revenue systems, including sales, billing, and revenue recognition. Ensure accurate financial reporting through close collaboration with the accounting team.

  • Collaborate with integration stakeholders (e.g., VBO, Workday, Sales and Revenue Engine (SRE)) to identify root causes of system failures and drive process improvements to enhance stability and performance. 

  • Develop reports for product and sales to drive forecasting and insights into the product. 

  • Actively coach and develop team members for performance management and personal/career growth in line with the values, expectations and culture of D&B.

• Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance or related fields

  • At least 10 years of experience in quota-to-cash, specifically with Revenue Cloud systems, preferably with 5 years at managerial capacity

  • Deep understanding of accounting and controls

  • Be adaptive to changes in fast-paced environment; Strong attention to detail, as well as being a problem solver who takes the initiative

  • Good communications skills with different levels

  • Analytical, result-oriented and organized

  • Be independent and flexible with strong business acumen

  • Able to work under pressure and tight schedules, willing to work outside normal working hours during close periods

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suites Skills

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of  driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

  • Willingness to travel and support events outside standard business hours as required

• Ability to sit, speak and operate telephone and/or computer for long periods of time

  • Ability to handle pressure, stressful conditions, and conflict resolution

  • Ability to work day, evening and/or weekend hours as needed

  • Ability to travel by any form of transportation

  • Ability to stand, walk, climb, kneel, crouch and bend over for work at office premises and office events

  • Regular attendance in the office 

    •  
     
    #LI-DNI

Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

We are looking for a Senior Manager to become a member of our Global Workplace Management Team. This role will be responsible for leading the facilities department and managing facilities operations for all North American activities. This is a leadership role that will drive a team to advocate for the development, implementation, maintenance, physical operational integration, and custodial control of all North American facilities with direct responsibility for the Jacksonville HQ.

The Senior Manager, Workplace Management will manage both the hard and soft services to ensure that team members are safe, secure, and comfortable and our core business is well supported. Planning, prioritizing and coordinating with all business partners are the fundamental skills you are bringing to drive forward the facilities function for D&B’s operations in North America.

lists.content:
• Leading and managing the North America Facilities service teams (internal and external) to ensure that the office operates smoothly and at optimal efficiency

  • Contract Management at local level monitoring performance and review KPI’s

  • Review & monitor Planned Preventative Maintenance (PPM) schedule ensuring adherence

  • Owning the relationship with building/property management, leadership, and team members

  • Landlord liaison to assure the quality of landlord related maintenance & repair and security services

  • Main point of contact for all suppliers - supervise and lead maintenance contractors, reviewing their scope of services, as required

  • Provides direction/guidance to the facilities staff, vendors, and service providers as required; ensure excellent coordination/execution of work with minimal disruption to operations, not limited to janitorial, food and vending services, building maintenance and grounds.

  • Accountable for the management of safe system of work for all contractors on site, issue, and review of permits to work and inspection of work pre and post service/repair

  • Overall responsibility for ensuring the office is compliant with health, safety, and legislation

  • Anticipate and implement conference and event solutions that meet the requirements of the business

  • Involvement in office related projects from conception to completion

  • Evaluate, plan, and allocate workspace to maximize capacity and utilization for changing team formation

  • Partners with Director of Physical Security to develop and implement Emergency Response Efforts, coordination of personnel, space, and equipment, advance efforts for the prevention and or mitigation of security incidents, and help determine appropriate responses (Site Leadership notification, CMT activation, etc.)

  • Supervises the preparation and submission of team’s requisitions and purchase orders; partners with Finance and Sourcing and Procurement to ensure timely payment processing and reconciliation of invoices, monthly accruals, reforecasts, and variance reports required by Finance.

  • Drive facilities ESG initiatives by researching, developing, and implementing measures to reduce waste, improve relationship/partnerships, and support D&B’s governance policies.  Ensure facilities EST related data collections are performed timely and inputted into our central platform.

• Relevant experience in similar role for 5+ years in facilities management, operational and/or managerial roles

  • Experience in implementing Health, Safety & Quality management policies and procedures to ISO standards & legislation

  • Project Management experience preferred

  • Professional membership in relevant body would be beneficial

  • Understanding of managing local budgets

  • Service orientated and a team player

  • Strongly motivated to deliver a world class standard of support and guidance to the business

  • Strong planning, organizational and problem-solving skills.

  • Excellent communication skills (both verbal & written)

  • Reliable, responsible and responsive person, who will bring professional approach to the role is essential

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite skills

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do. Be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action. Be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

The Senior Principal Data Scientist - AI Lead will be the point of contact for Public Sector / Government stakeholders and will be responsible for end-to-end AI solution delivery.
 
This role will partner with U.S. government agencies on programs that depend on trusted entity data, validation, and risk insight.
description:
Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

The Senior Principal Data Scientist - AI Lead will be the point of contact for Public Sector / Government stakeholders and will be responsible for end-to-end AI solution delivery.
 
This role will partner with U.S. government agencies on programs that depend on trusted entity data, validation, and risk insight.

lists.content:
• Data expertise: Strong skills in data analysis, manipulation, and processing for training and evaluating AI models.

• Experience leading large-scale digital transformations: In IT Operations for global

• 10+ years of professional Data science / ML/AI development experience with a preference working with Government / public sector clients

• Deep understanding of AI/ML Technologies: Solid grasp of machine learning, deep learning, NLP, and hands-on experience on AI automation.

• Leadership and team management: Proven ability to lead and mentor teams, foster innovation, and manage the full software development lifecycle.

• Strong programming skills: Expertise in languages like Python, Java, or Go, essential for developing and deploying AI solutions.

• Cloud platform knowledge: Familiarity with AWS, Azure, or GCP for deploying and scaling AI applications.

• Strategic thinking and vision: Capability to identify opportunities, define technical roadmaps, and align AI solutions with business objectives.

• Excellent communication and collaboration: The ability to communicate complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders and foster cross-functional teamwork.

• Problem-solving and critical thinking: Exceptional analytical skills to identify challenges, develop innovative solutions, and optimize system performance.

• Adaptability and continuous learning: Commitment to staying updated with the rapidly evolving AI landscape and embracing new technologies.

• Ethical considerations and bias mitigation: Understanding and addressing the ethical implications of AI, ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability.

• Significant experience in IT and solution architecture roles: with a focus on automation, infrastructure, or cloud.

• Proven track record of designing and implementing AI/ML solutions: Especially in IT operations, infrastructure management, or business process automation.

• Bachelors Degree - Required
  • Master and/or PhD Degree - Highly Preferred

The Senior Principal Product Manager, Go to Market (GTM) sets the enterprise level growth strategy for complex, multi region product launches and commercialization. This role defines and governs the end to end GTM vision, shaping market positioning, accelerating adoption, and driving sustained revenue impact.
 
The Senior Principal Product Manager, Go to Market (GTM) influences executive decision making and aligns senior leaders across product, commercial, regional, and external partner ecosystems to ensure consistent global execution. Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

The Senior Principal Product Manager, Go to Market (GTM) sets the enterprise level growth strategy for complex, multi region product launches and commercialization. This role defines and governs the end to end GTM vision, shaping market positioning, accelerating adoption, and driving sustained revenue impact.
 
The Senior Principal Product Manager, Go to Market (GTM) influences executive decision making and aligns senior leaders across product, commercial, regional, and external partner ecosystems to ensure consistent global execution. The position acts as a recognized industry thought leader, representing the Risk portfolio externally while translating emerging market, regulatory, and technology trends into long term business opportunities for Dun & Bradstreet.

lists.content:
• Set and advance enterprise‑level growth opportunities by shaping unclear, emerging market, regulatory, and customer challenges into aligned, executive‑ready strategies and investment cases.

  • Serve as a recognized industry authority and external thought leader, defining a narrative and influencing market direction through press, white papers, events, webinars, and senior‑level forums.

  • Own and govern the full product commercialization lifecycle across regions—from GTM vision through scaled adoption—holding accountability for outcomes across EAPs, pilots, and general availability.

  • Define and steward comprehensive, multi‑region go‑to‑market strategies, including target segments, positioning, value messaging, pricing models, and channel approach, ensuring consistency and local relevance.

  • Drive cross‑functional and regional alignment at the senior‑leader level, influencing priorities and execution across product, marketing, sales, finance, operations, and partners without direct authority.

  • Establish and monitor enterprise‑level success metrics, including adoption, revenue, market share, acquisition, and retention; course‑correct strategy based on performance, market signal, and customer insight.

  • Partner with senior marketing and sales leadership to ensure scalable, high‑impact GTM enablement, shaping messaging frameworks, sales narratives, and executive‑level customer materials.

  • Act as the strategic voice of the customer and market, providing forward‑looking, data‑driven insights and recommendations that inform executive decision‑making and long‑term portfolio direction.

• Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent experience demonstrating enterprise‑level impact.

  • Recognized industry authority with deep domain expertise and external credibility among senior customers, partners, and industry peers.

  • Minimum 15+ years of B2B experience shaping go‑to‑market and product strategies for analytics‑led solutions within KYC, KYB, Regulatory, Risk, or Supply domains.

  • Demonstrated fluency in competitive dynamics and market structures across North America and Europe, with experience operating on a regional or global scale.

  • Proven record of owning, governing, and scaling complex product launches and GTM strategies with sustained revenue and market impact.

  • Advanced capability in market intelligence, competitive analysis, and customer insight, with a strategic understanding of full product lifecycle and customer journeys.

  • Highly analytical and outcomes‑driven, with the ability to prioritize enterprise‑level initiatives, operate with ambiguity, and maintain strong ownership and execution discipline.

  • Exceptional executive‑level communication, presentation, and influencing skills; fluent in English, with additional regional language capabilities where applicable.

The Senior Principal Solutions Architect function leads one or more workstreams in complex enterprise client engagements, partnering with key collaborators to drive and achieve the vision while completing key deliverables.

The Senior Principal Solutions Architect as hands-on advisors, using strong interpersonal skills, solution and/or technical knowledge, Dun & Bradstreet solutions knowledge, industry experience, consulting experience, and knowledge of their client’s business to deliver customer return on investment.

lists.content:
• Minimum of 15 years prior experience in leading the architecture and implementation of enterprise scale integration solutions composed from a diverse range of technologies, integration patterns, approaches and deployment models.

• Industry specific domain knowledge which has been applied to tangibly increasing the value of a solution implemented for a client.

• Deep understanding of modern technical architecture, including cloud infrastructure and applications.

• Proficiency in data integration using and deploying API’s.

• Highly articulate with excellent business communication (verbal and written) skills and presentation skills suitable for a global corporate environment.

• Wiling to travel beyond city limits for the interest of business.

• Possesses excellent MS-Excel, MS-PowerPoint and MS-Word skills.

• Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

• Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholder, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

• Drive significant business outcomes for client by enabling them to optimize their adoption of Dun & Bradstreet solutions across their ecosystem and complex integration landscapes.

• Translate business requirements into well-architected solutions.

• Lead technical design sessions; architect and document technical solutions aligned with client business objectives; identify gaps between client's current and desired end states Interact with client product owner, business process owner and technical resources to understand requirements, analyze information, and craft comprehensive solutions.

• Maintain a strong understanding of typical business challenges or common objectives faced by clients within the industry.

• Support paid services engagements, delivering clear and compelling design documentation to enable customer implementation success.

• This role is intended for a professional who can apply broad expertise and knowledge.

The Solutions Architecture function leads one or more workstreams in complex enterprise client engagements, partnering with key collaborators to drive and achieve the vision while completing key deliverables. 

The Senior Principal Solutions Architect provides hands-on advisors, using strong interpersonal skills, solution and/or technical knowledge, Dun & Bradstreet solutions knowledge, industry experience, consulting experience, and knowledge of their client’s business to deliver customer return on investment. 

The Senior Principal Solutions Architects apply hands-on experience with modern data, analytics, AI, and cloud technologies—including building AI-driven, agentic, and automated solutions—to design and deliver scalable architectures that demonstrate the value of D&B data across diverse client use cases.

lists.content:
• Minimum of 15 years prior experience in leading the architecture and implementation of enterprise-scale integration solutions composed from a diverse range of technologies, integration patterns, approaches and deployment models 
• Industry specific domain knowledge which has been applied to tangibly increasing the value of a solution implemented for a client 
• Deep understanding of modern technical architecture, including cloud infrastructure and applications  
• Proficiency in data integration using and deploying API’s  
• Hands-on expertise with modern data, analytics, and visualization tools (e.g., SQL, Python, Power BI, Tableau, Snowflake, Databricks)  
• Hands-on expertise architecting AI/ML, agentic, and automated solutions (e.g., LLMs, RAG, MCP, agent frameworks, workflow automation) at enterprise scale  
• Preferred – Cloud certifications at the Professional or Specialty level (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect Professional, Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect)  
• Preferred – AI/ML or specialty certifications (e.g., AWS ML Specialty, Azure AI Engineer, Databricks ML Professional)  
• Preferred – Data management, integration, or governance certifications (e.g., DAMA-CDMP, MuleSoft, Snowflake)  
• Highly articulate with excellent business communication (verbal and written) skills and presentation skills suitable for a global corporate environment  
• Wiling to travel beyond city limits for the interest of business  
• Possesses excellent MS-Excel, MS-PowerPoint and MS-Word skills  
• Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success  
• Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholder, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs

• Drive significant business outcomes for client by enabling them to optimize their adoption of Dun & Bradstreet solutions across their ecosystem and complex integration landscapes.

• Translate business requirements into well-architected solutions.

• Lead technical design sessions; architect and document technical solutions aligned with client business objectives; identify gaps between client's current and desired end states Interact with client product owner, business process owner and technical resources to understand requirements, analyze information, and craft comprehensive solutions.

• Interact with client product owner, business process owner and technical resources to understand requirements, analyze information, and craft comprehensive solutions  

• Maintain a strong understanding of typical business challenges or common objectives faced by clients within the industry.

• Support paid services engagements, delivering clear and compelling design documentation to enable customer implementation success.

• Define and architect AI-driven, agentic, and automated solutions at enterprise scale; provide hands-on technical leadership in prototyping, validating, and developing reference architectures 

• Apply broad hands-on expertise across cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and modern data, analytics, and visualization tools to architect and deliver complex, multi-domain client solutions 

• This role is intended for a professional who can apply broad expertise and knowledge.

• Possesses excellent MS-Excel, MS-PowerPoint and MS-Word skills.

• Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

• Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholder, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

• Drive significant business outcomes for client by enabling them to optimize their adoption of Dun & Bradstreet solutions across their ecosystem and complex integration landscapes.

• Translate business requirements into well-architected solutions.

• Lead technical design sessions; architect and document technical solutions aligned with client business objectives; identify gaps between client's current and desired end states Interact with client product owner, business process owner and technical resources to understand requirements, analyze information, and craft comprehensive solutions.

• Maintain a strong understanding of typical business challenges or common objectives faced by clients within the industry.

• Support paid services engagements, delivering clear and compelling design documentation to enable customer implementation success.

• This role is intended for a professional who can apply broad expertise and knowledge.

This role involves driving product vision, market analysis, and cross-functional collaboration to deliver innovative solutions that meet customer needs and align with business goals.

description:
Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

The Senior Product Manager will lead the strategy, development, and management of AI-native products within our portfolio. This role involves driving product vision, market analysis, and cross-functional collaboration to deliver innovative solutions that meet customer needs and align with business goals.

The Senior Product Manager will be responsible for overseeing the product lifecycle from ideation through to launch and beyond, ensuring the product’s success in the market.

lists.content:
• Product management function participates in the development of document specifications that outline revisions or additional features of existing products, and the conception of new products.
  • Completes market research to identify trends and/or needs of targeted clients/customers. Analyzes and compares competitor's product lines in relation to the organization's current offerings. 
  • Develop strategies for competitive positioning to increase sales and promotions.
  • Serves as a liaison between other departments to gather client/customer feedback and disseminate information about the status of product development.
  • The ideal candidate will combine strong product leadership with hands-on fluency in AI capabilities and a passion for leveraging trusted data to power intelligent, differentiated solutions.
  • Be a subject matter expert on D&B enterprise AI capabilities and how they can be leveraged to fulfill a specific solution.
  • Develop and maintain a product roadmap that aligns with business goals and customer needs. Manage risks & issues and execute on product build.
  • Manage the product throughout its lifecycle, from ideation to retirement. Prioritize product features and enhancements based on strategic goals. Ensuring continuous improvement and innovation.
  • Collaborate with cross functional teams including GTM, Technology, Sales, Customer Experience, Solution Architecture, Pricing and Finance to define product requirements and ensure successful development and delivery.
  • Act as the voice of the customer within the organization, ensuring that products meet quality standards, user needs, and expectations.
  • Keep abreast of latest market trends, perform research to understand customers’ needs, industry trends and competition landscape.
  • Lead the product development process, collaborating with engineering, design, and other teams to deliver high-quality products on time.
  • Proactively seeks feedback with sellers on product pipeline ensuring adoption, retention and growth.
  • Create detailed product requirements, user stories, and specifications.
  • Define and track key product metrics to measure product performance, trends, and risks to inform data driven decisions about areas for improvement.
  • Develop business cases for new product initiatives.
  • Provide mentorship and guidance to junior product managers and team members.

• Bachelor’s Degree with major in Business Administration, Technology or related disciplines.

  • At least 8 years’ experience of pre-sales / solutions consulting/ product management/ project management.

  • Strong understanding of market research, competitive analysis, and customer needs assessment.

  • Proven track record of managing all aspects of a successful product throughout its lifecycle.

  • Analytical capabilities, results oriented, with proven ability to multitask and prioritize.

  • Excellent communication and presentation skill, with experience in dealing with senior management and all levels including across matrix teams.

  • Independent, detailed oriented, and a good team player.

  • Ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams including marketing, sales and analytics teams.

  • Ability to translate business goals into product requirements.

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite skills.

  • Proficiency in leveraging AI tools and capabilities (e.g., generative AI, copilots, agent frameworks) in day-to-day workflows to enhance productivity, research, and product development.

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do. Be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action. Be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

We’re looking for a commercially minded Senior Product Manager (GTM) to lead how our global finance & credit products are brought to market across multiple countries.
In this role, you’ll own the end-to-end go-to-market strategy, shaping how we position, launch, and scale our products to drive strong adoption and revenue growth. You’ll work closely with senior stakeholders across product, sales, marketing, and customer teams to ensure every launch lands with impact.
If you enjoy turning great products into successful commercial outcomes and thrive in a fast-moving, cross-functional environment, this is a role where you can make a real difference.

We’re looking for a commercially minded Senior Product Manager (GTM) to lead how our global finance & credit products are brought to market across multiple countries.

In this role, you’ll own the end-to-end go-to-market strategy, shaping how we position, launch, and scale our products to drive strong adoption and revenue growth. You’ll work closely with senior stakeholders across product, sales, marketing, and customer teams to ensure every launch lands with impact.

If you enjoy turning great products into successful commercial outcomes and thrive in a fast-moving, cross-functional environment, this is a role where you can make a real difference.

lists.text: What you will be responsible for:
lists.content:
• Own and deliver go-to-market strategy: Define how products are positioned, priced, and launched to win in the market
  • Drive successful product launches: Lead cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, high-impact launches across regions
  • Accelerate adoption and revenue growth: Track performance and continuously optimise GTM strategies to maximise commercial success
  • Enable sales to succeed: Equip commercial teams with compelling messaging, demos, and materials that convert
  • Partner with marketing to build demand: Shape campaigns and content that drive awareness, engagement, and pipeline
  • Use data to refine strategy: Analyse market performance, customer feedback, and competitor activity to stay ahead
  • Own product performance in market: Monitor key metrics such as revenue, customer acquisition, and retention
  • Act as the voice of the customer: Ensure product and positioning align with real customer needs and expectations
  • Influence at senior level: Provide clear, data-led insights on performance, opportunities, and ROI
  • Support and mentor others: Share your experience to help develop junior team members and strengthen GTM capability

• Proven experience leading go-to-market strategies and product launches in a B2B environment, with around 8+ years of experience working across product management, GTM or similar
  • Strong background in data, analytics, or technology-led products
  • Ability to translate market insight into clear commercial strategy and results
  • Experience working closely with sales, marketing, and product teams in a cross-functional setting
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to turn data into action
  • Confident communicator, able to influence stakeholders at all levels
  • Highly organised, able to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
  • A proactive, ownership-driven approach: you spot opportunities and take action
  • Degree in Business, Marketing, Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

· Up to 16 weeks 100% paid parental leave after one year of employment.
· Paid sick time to care for yourself or family members. 
· Education assistance and extensive training resources.
· Do Good Program: Paid volunteer days & donation matching.  
· Competitive 401k with company matching. 
· Health & wellness benefits, including discounted Wellhub membership rates.
· Medical, dental & vision insurance for you, spouse/partner & dependents.

The Senior Marketing Manager, Product Marketing plays a critical role in shaping how Dun & Bradstreet’s solutions are understood, valued, and talked about in the market. description:
Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

The Senior Marketing Manager, Product Marketing plays a critical role in shaping how Dun & Bradstreet’s solutions are understood, valued, and talked about in the market. This role sits within the Marketing organization and focuses on market insight, customer understanding, positioning, and narrative development, rather than pricing, packaging, or commercial go to market ownership.

This role partners closely with Product, Portfolio Marketing, Content, Demand Generation, Sales Enablement, and broader Marketing teams to challenge assumptions, elevate messaging quality, and ensure customer centric thinking is embedded across programs and communication.

lists.content:
• Act as a deep subject matter and buyer expert for assigned solutions, industries, or use cases, building strong understanding of customer needs, buying motivations, pain points, and decision criteria.

  • Develop and continuously refine positioning frameworks, value propositions, and core narratives that articulate differentiated value in a clear, credible, and customer centric way.

  • Lead market, customer, and persona research (qualitative and quantitative), synthesizing insights into clear implications for messaging, content, and programs.

  • Serve as a strategic thought partner to Product, Portfolio Marketing, GTM, Product Excellence and Demand Generation-constructively challenging inputs, assumptions, and priorities to improve outcomes.

  • Translate complex product capabilities into clear, compelling, and audience relevant narratives, working in close alignment with Creative & Brand as well as External Communications teams to ensure consistency of voice, positioning, and storytelling across the buyer journey.

  • Partner with Sales Enablement to help customer facing teams understand buyer context, value articulation, and objection handling, without owning pricing or packaging.

  • Support launches and major initiatives by shaping the story and messaging, while execution and orchestration remain with GTM and demand teams.

  • Actively identify gaps, inconsistencies, or missed opportunities in how solutions are communicated and proactively propose improvements.

  • Measure and evaluate the effectiveness of messaging and content, using insights to iterate and raise the overall quality bar.

  • Present messaging, insights, and recommendations confidently to stakeholders at multiple levels.

  • Perform writing, editing, and special project work as needed.

• Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business or a related field.

  • 8+ years of experience in product marketing, portfolio marketing or strategic marketing within B2B environments.

  • Experience working with sales and marketing solutions (e.g., CRM, marketing automation, customer data, analytics, enablement platforms), with an understanding of how these tools are evaluated, positioned, and used by commercial teams.

  • Strong writing, editing, and storytelling skills across multiple content formats, including SEO optimized digital content, with the ability to create compelling narratives and messaging frameworks for both executive and practitioner audiences.

  • Data- and performance-metrics-driven, with demonstrated experience using insights and analysis to inform decisions and turn complexity into clarity.

  • Experience self-publishing website content using Adobe Experience Manager is a strong plus.

  • Solid grounding in core marketing disciplines, including market research, positioning, segmentation, personal development, messaging, and storytelling.

  • Experience working in matrixed, cross-functional organizations, influencing outcomes without formal authority.

  • Confident and persuasive communicator with strong written and verbal skills and a clear point of view.

  • Comfortable acting as a constructive challenger—asking “why,” identifying gaps, and pushing for stronger thinking.

  • Highly proactive and self-directed, anticipating needs, prioritizing by impact versus effort, and operating without constant direction.

  • Proven experience collaborating closely with Product, Sales, Content, and Demand teams.

  • Proficient with standard marketing and collaboration tools.

  • Hands-on exposure to sales workflows and marketing execution, enabling messaging and narratives grounded in real-world commercial use cases.

  • Experience using AI-enabled tools as part of the daily marketing workflow (e.g., research, insight synthesis, content drafting, analysis, ideation, optimization).

  • Strong understanding of how AI can augment marketing effectiveness and productivity, coupled with sound judgment around quality control, human oversight, and responsible use

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suites Skills

  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.

  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.

  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

• Ability to sit, speak and operate telephone and/or computer for long periods of time

  • Ability to handle pressure, stressful conditions, and conflict resolution

  • Ability to work day, evening and/or weekend hours as needed

lists.content:
• Lead the development of integrated marketing strategies that align with client business objectives and long‑term growth priorities.
  • Optimize end‑to‑end marketing spend by recommending the most relevant solutions from the D&B Bouquet across markets, customer segments, and channels. Act as a trusted advisor to CMOs and senior stakeholders, guiding them on budget planning, investment optimization, and strategic decision‑making.
  • Define clear market and account strategies for both new and existing clients to drive sustained business impact.
  • Oversee the onboarding of new accounts by setting strategic direction, establishing success metrics, and building strong operational frameworks.
  • Mentor, coach, and guide team members to ensure high standards in strategic planning, execution quality, and client delivery
  • Support organizational growth initiatives by contributing to pitch development, solution design, and the acquisition of new strategic accounts.

• Experience: 6+ years of experience in marketing strategy, spend allocation, and account planning
  • Proven CMO-level stakeholder management experience
  • Background in media, marketing, consulting, or strategy-led environments
  • Team leadership experience with strong commercial acumen and analytical skills

At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.


The Role: We are seeking a highly skilled Full Stack Engineer to design, develop, and scale next-generation Data Quality and AI-enabled platforms. In this role, you will work across the entire technology stack—from intuitive user experiences and data visualizations to cloud-native backend services and data processing pipelines. You will partner closely with Data Quality SMEs, Data Engineers, Product Managers, Data Scientists, and Platform Teams to build solutions that support data observability, analytics, data governance, and AI-driven insights.
You will help build the next generation of D&B's Data Quality Insights platform, enabling data governance, observability, analytics, and AI-powered decision support for enterprise data products.
description:
Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.

The Role: We are seeking a highly skilled Full Stack Engineer to design, develop, and scale next-generation Data Quality and AI-enabled platforms. In this role, you will work across the entire technology stack—from intuitive user experiences and data visualizations to cloud-native backend services and data processing pipelines. You will partner closely with Data Quality SMEs, Data Engineers, Product Managers, Data Scientists, and Platform Teams to build solutions that support data observability, analytics, data governance, and AI-driven insights.

You will help build the next generation of D&B's Data Quality Insights platform, enabling data governance, observability, analytics, and AI-powered decision support for enterprise data products.

lists.content:
Full stack Application Development

  • Design and develop modern, responsive web applications and drive the end-to-end development of scalable full-stack solutions using React, TypeScript, and Python (FastAPI), delivering high-performance web applications, APIs, microservices, analytics dashboards, data platform integrations, and AI-powered capabilities in partnership with Product, Data Engineering, Architecture, and Platform teams.

    • Data Quality & Analytics

    • Build and enhance next-generation Data Quality and Governance platform that enables rule management, observability, metadata-driven monitoring, policy enforcement, and AI-powered insights. Develop scalable capabilities (front-end and back-end) for data quality assessment, anomaly detection, lineage, governance workflows, and self-service analytics, transforming business requirements into extensible enterprise-grade solutions.

      • Cloud Engineering & DevOps

      • Build cloud-native applications using GCP. Containerize applications using GKE and orchestrate deployments through CI/CD pipelines, preferably Terraform. Implement Infrastructure-as-Code and engineering best practices. Participate in operational excellence activities, monitoring, reliability, and platform optimization.

        • AI & automation

        • Integrate Generative AI and Agentic AI capabilities into engineering solutions. Collaborate with Data Scientists and AI teams to operationalize AI/ML models. Develop AI-powered workflows, anomaly detection solutions, and intelligent user experiences. Build and integrate LLM-enabled services, copilots, and automation capabilities where appropriate.

          • Engineering Governance

          • Write clean, maintainable, and testable code. Develop automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests. Perform code reviews, technical documentation and contribute to engineering standards and best practices. Champion security, scalability, performance, and accessibility standards. Participate in architectural discussions and comply to Agile/Scrum practices.

• 5-8 years of professional full‑stack/software engineering development experience with demonstrated ownership of end‑to‑end systems.
  • Strong experience in modern frontend development using React/Angular, TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5/CSS3
  • Strong backend development experience using Python, FastAPI or Flask
  • Strong SQL skills with experience developing analytical queries against large-scale datasets.
  • Experience designing and developing REST APIs, Microservices architectures, Event-driven systems
  • Experience with databases PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB or equivalent NoSQL technologies
  • Experience with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, Terraform, Git-based development workflows
  • Experience building and deploying applications on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) including BigQuery, Composer (Airflow), Cloud Storage, Pub/Sub, Dataproc, Dataflow and GKE. Experience with Terraform and Infrastructure-as-Code is highly preferred.
  • Strong testing fundamentals across the stack (unit, integration, E2E).
  • Solid understanding of Git workflows and development tooling (npm/yarn etc).
  • Familiarity with data pipelines, ETL/ELT processing, metadata-driven architectures, observability frameworks, and large-scale analytical platforms.
  • Strong grounding in web security, including authentication, authorization, and secure API communication.
  • Strong problem-solving, communication, and collaboration skills.
  • Ability to work independently and in cross-functional teams.
  • Focus on quality, performance, and maintainability.

As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers.
descriptionBody:
The Role: We are seeking a highly skilled Full Stack Engineer to design, develop, and scale next-generation Data Quality and AI-enabled platforms. In this role, you will work across the entire technology stack—from intuitive user experiences and data visualizations to cloud-native backend services and data processing pipelines. You will partner closely with Data Quality SMEs, Data Engineers, Product Managers, Data Scientists, and Platform Teams to build solutions that support data observability, analytics, data governance, and AI-driven insights.

You will help build the next generation of D&B's Data Quality Insights platform, enabling data governance, observability, analytics, and AI-powered decision support for enterprise data products.
descriptionBodyPlain:
The Role: We are seeking a highly skilled Full Stack Engineer to design, develop, and scale next-generation Data Quality and AI-enabled platforms. You will partner closely with Data Quality SMEs, Data Engineers, Product Managers, Data Scientists, and Platform Teams to build solutions that support data observability, analytics, data governance, and AI-driven insights.
You will help build the next generation of D&B's Data Quality Insights platform, enabling data governance, observability, analytics, and AI-powered decision support for enterprise data products.

descriptionPlain: The Senior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) is responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, performance, and operability of production systems across our platforms, by applying software engineering practices to operations, with a focus on automation, observability, and incident response.
description: The Senior Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) is responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, performance, and operability of production systems across our platforms, by applying software engineering practices to operations, with a focus on automation, observability, and incident response.

lists.content:
• Own and improve the reliability, availability, and performance of production services in Google Cloud (GCP).
  • Participate in incident management, including detection, triage, mitigation, escalation, and recovery.
  • Use and improve incident workflows and tooling (e.g., ServiceNow) to ensure clear ownership and timely communication.
  • Design, implement, and operate observability solutions including monitoring, logging, tracing, synthetics, and dashboards (e.g., Splunk Observability, OpenTelemetry).
  • Reduce operational toil through automation and engineering-led solutions, proactively introducing and driving SRE best practices.
  • Support on-call rotations across multiple time zones, contributing to a sustainable 24/7 support model.
  • Define, monitor, and report SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets for critical services.
  • Drive and be accountable for best-in-class service availability through SRE principles, automation, and proactive reliability engineering.

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or related field
  • Strong experience with cloud-native concepts and technologies, with a strong preference for Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Kubernetes (GKE).
  • Proven experience with Site Reliability Engineering and production incident management, ideally using platforms such as ServiceNow.
  • Experience with monitoring and observability tools, including metrics, logs, traces, and synthetics (e.g., Splunk Observability, OpenTelemetry).
  • Exposure to reliability testing, resilience engineering, or cost optimisation initiatives.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to diagnose complex production issues quickly.
  • Software development or automation experience using Python, shell scripts, or similar languages.
  • Hands-on experience operating production cloud infrastructure at scale.
  • Experience managing multi-region, high-availability production systems with a focus on scalability, resilience, and minimising service disruption during failures.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suites Skills
  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do; be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action, be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.
  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.
  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. Sr. Software Engineer – Duties are designing & developing service-oriented & resource-oriented software architecture using Python in Flask framework for e-commerce systems for scalable web-centric AWS & GCP application platforms & frameworks using SOAP & REST protocols & server-side GitHub REST API including performing requirements analysis; reviewing designs for new functionality; formulating detailed functional & product specifications; collaborating with cross-functional finance & product team leads; performing requirements functional decomposition, unit & integration testing; supporting technology upgrades through cloud migrations; performing SQL & NoSQL database tuning & maintenance; and providing technical documentation & system implementation support.  Position requires Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems or a related field & 5 yrs exp in job duties as stated.  Position is with Dun & Bradstreet located in Jacksonville, FL. Inquire and send resume through Dun & Bradstreet’s job board at https://jobs.lever.co/dnb. Position is under Senior Software Engineer for Jacksonville, FL.
 
#LI-DNI

You will be responsible for designing and developing modern, cutting-edge RESTful APIs. You’ll be working in a stimulating, collaborative environment focused on continuous improvement, innovation and growth. You will be passionate about software engineering, excited by the possibilities of technology to transform industries, and inspired by the power of data and AI to create meaningful outcomes.

lists.text: As part of the Prime team, you’ll:
lists.content:
• Be an integral member of a cross-functional team in an agile environment
  • Develop platform capabilities to meet business requirements
  • Determine operational feasibility of proposed solutions
  • Validate solutions using proof of concepts and prototypes
  • Write simple code (low maintenance, extensibility, testability and performance)
  • Collaborate with the team to take advantage of new concepts/technologies
  • Make informed decisions quickly and taking ownership of services at scale
  • Explore practical uses of AI-assisted engineering tools to improve developer productivity, code quality and delivery efficiency
  • Mentoring and supporting junior members of the teams

• You will have a bachelor’s degree in computer science or related field
  • You will have around 6+ years of experience developing commercial software
  • You will have experience working on Web Service APIs using Java and Spring
  • You will have experience delivering solutions on GCP or AWS utilizing a broad set of the services available
  • You have worked in a collaborative setting
  • You will be a problem solver
  • You demonstrate humility, empathy and support for your colleagues
  • You are curious about emerging technologies, including AI-assisted development, and apply sound engineering judgment when adopting new tools

    •

Shape the Future with Dun & Bradstreet
At Dun & Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. The Solution Sales function is responsible for driving growth in specific lines of business. Interpreting and defining client needs in order to align the client’s objectives and requirements with Dun & Bradstreet’s products’ capabilities and value. 
 
In this role, you will work in partnership with the Relationship Manager and Client Success team, act as a senior specialist in our product offerings, supporting the Relationship Manager by conducting live product demonstrations via web-based meetings, in-person meetings, and through providing collateral to support driving Cross Sell, Upsell and New Business.

lists.content:
• Fulfill the role of trusted advisor on Dun & Bradstreet solutions through strategic conversations to understand organizational business objectives and goals.
  • Working in conjunction with a frontline sales team, actively manage the evaluation stage of the sales process and serve as the technical advisor for Dun & Bradstreet’s product suite. 
  • Assist with client facing demonstrations by clearly articulating underlying methodologies, while providing guidance on how the product functionality helps to improve organizational efficiencies.
  • Actively participate in account planning for assigned accounts, provide industry trends and assist with client/market segmentation of Dun & Bradstreet’s products.
  • Capture solution insights based on competitive analysis and client meetings; share insights with internal product teams during product design and development cycles.
  • Mapping out of customer use case journeys, identifying pain points, and proposing solutions by conducting product demonstrations, workshops, and POCs to showcase the capabilities and value of our solutions.
  • Be a respected subject matter expert for assigned solution(s); discuss product strategy and roadmaps in the context of market and technology trends.
  • Demonstrate product capabilities, overcome client objections and create a client specific buying vision.
  • Maintain and grow revenue stream through successful interpreting and defining client needs to align the client’s objectives and business requirements with Dun & Bradstreet’s products’ capabilities and value.
  • Support sellers in effectively packaging, pricing and presenting solutions to clients including return on investment.
  • Maintain consistent and accurate data in relevant CRM software (e.g. SFDC) and other sales tools to support territory, account planning and forecasting.
  • Ensure compliance with all business initiatives.
  • Serve as a thought leader in the industry, staying up-to-date with market trends, competitors, and emerging technologies.
  • Attend industry events, conferences, and trade shows to network and promote D&B's offerings.

• Minimum of five (5) years prior experience in customer facing Field sales role selling new solutions to clients and generating significant revenue growth.
  • Customer centric with a proactive attitude to tackle problems and deliver solutions for great customer experience at scale for large companies during the whole customer journey
  • Getting information on client’s decision criteria and executing tailored activities to meet them
  • Experienced communicator when managing customers at different organisational levels.
  • High aptitude to stay current and train on related technology areas
  • Adept at providing technical solutions based on clients’ needs per the practice area
  • Proficiency in CRM software (e.g., SFDC) and Microsoft Office Suite skills
  • Compliance with industry professional certifications is required when selling specific regulated financial or data products
  • Able and willing to mentor and support other members of the team
  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do. Be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action. Be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.
  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.
  • Fluency in German and English

Role Summary: The Senior Team Lead will be responsible for delivering impact assessment reports, white papers, and revenue-generating research studies. The incumbent will lead Economic and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) projects, ensuring timely delivery, methodological rigor, and client satisfaction. The role involves conducting research, performing data analysis, developing high-quality reports, and ensuring rigorous quality reviews. The individual will work closely with internal and external stakeholders to understand business requirements, provide strategic recommendations, and drive thought leadership and business growth through actionable insights.
 
 
Work Location: 4 days per week from the BKC (Mumbai) office and 1 day per week from the Turbhe (Navi Mumbai) office. Candidates should be comfortable traveling to both locations.

lists.content:
• Serve as the primary interface for internal sales teams and external stakeholders to drive growth in Economic Impact Assessment, Social Impact Assessment, CSR Impact Assessment, and Policy Evaluation services. Lead client discussions, identify and qualify opportunities, develop tailored proposals and methodologies, support solutioning, and convert prospects into long-term client engagements.
  • Lead the end-to-end execution of impact assessment mandates, including research design, data collection, stakeholder consultations, economic and social impact modelling, policy analysis, report writing, quality assurance, and client presentations. Ensure timely delivery of high-quality, insight-driven reports and actionable recommendations that meet client and stakeholder expectations.
  • Deliver in-depth macroeconomic and microeconomic analyses, offering a 360° perspective on India's growth trajectory, policy developments, and economic risks in a timely manner. Assess structural trends across key sectors such as agriculture, manufacturing, services, infrastructure, and the financial ecosystem, producing white papers and articles that offer unique insights.
  • Design and implement robust systems and models to forecast critical economic indicators, including GDP, inflation, trade balances, fiscal metrics, and sector-specific trends. Utilize advanced quantitative techniques to support predictive analytics and scenario building.
  • Leverage large datasets to uncover economic patterns and trends. Maintain, enhance, and validate complex economic databases, ensuring data integrity and accuracy to support analytical outputs.
  • Lead comprehensive sectoral studies covering rural consumption, production efficiencies, supply chain dynamics, credit flows, external trade, and financial market trends. Identify emerging risks and opportunities at both macro and micro levels.
  • Develop high-quality whitepapers, policy briefs, and opinion pieces that influence stakeholders across industries, financial institutions, and government bodies. Represent the group in internal & external forums, client briefings, media interactions, and time-critical insight requests, positioning the firm as a trusted voice on Indian economic matters.
  • Deliver timely, high-impact analyses on key economic events, data releases, and policy changes, ensuring that clients receive quick, responsive, and actionable insights.
  • Provide bespoke economic insights tailored to client needs, including risk assessments, investment analyses, and strategic recommendations. Engage directly with clients to present findings, address queries, and offer consultative expertise.

• Advanced degree in Economics (Master’s or Ph.D.) with a strong foundation in macroeconomics, econometrics, and quantitative analysis.
  • 10+ years of relevant experience in economic research, policy analysis, consulting, or financial services, with a proven track record of delivering impactful economic insights on India and global economies.
  • Deep understanding of India's economic structure, including rural and urban consumption trends, production ecosystems, external trade dynamics, credit and lending markets, and financial sector regulations.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, capable of translating complex economic concepts into clear, concise, and compelling narratives for diverse audiences, including media engagements.
  • Strong client-facing capabilities with experience in presenting economic insights to senior stakeholders, policymakers, and industry leaders.

text: Senior Team Lead - Economic Research & Advisory (R-19626)

descriptionBody:
Role Summary: The Senior Team Lead will be responsible for delivering impact assessment reports, white papers, and revenue-generating research studies. The incumbent will lead Economic and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) projects, ensuring timely delivery, methodological rigor, and client satisfaction. The role involves conducting research, performing data analysis, developing high-quality reports, and ensuring rigorous quality reviews. The individual will work closely with internal and external stakeholders to understand business requirements, provide strategic recommendations, and drive thought leadership and business growth through actionable insights.
 
 
Work Location: 4 days per week from the BKC (Mumbai) office and 1 day per week from the Turbhe (Navi Mumbai) office. Candidates should be comfortable traveling to both locations.

The Software Engineer II is responsible for designing, developing, and delivering scalable data platform solutions that support high-performance data processing and analytics capabilities. This role operates with increased autonomy, contributing to system design, driving delivery of platform capabilities, and ensuring the reliability and scalability of the Dun & Bradstreet data ecosystem.

The role plays a key part in modernizing and optimizing data platforms, including identity resolution and Match systems, to meet growing business demands.

lists.text: What’s on Offer at D&B Ireland
lists.content:
• 25 days annual leave (plus 2 paid volunteer days & 1 paid un-sick day)
  • Holiday buy & sell (the option to buy or sell up to 5 additional days per year)
  • Flexible working - hybrid model
  • Employee Health Insurance
  • Mental Health Support program
  • Pension Contribution
  • Family Friendly Leave (Maternity, Paternity, Parental, Marriage and Bereavement)
  • Life Assurance
  • Educational Assistance Program
  • Life-Style Account (D&B will match your contributions up to €40 per month and can be used to claim for a range of health-related, leisure or lifestyle activities)

    • At Dun & Bradstreet, we are 6,000 friendly colleagues around the world waiting to meet you and give you the opportunity to grow your career.

• Design, develop, test, deploy, and monitor scalable applications for data ingestion, mastering, matching, and publishing
  • Develop high-quality software using Java and Python, ensuring performance, maintainability, extensibility, and reliability.
  • Deliver end-to-end features across the software stack, operating with ownership of assigned components.
  • Build and maintain microservices and REST APIs, including real-time and streaming data capabilities.
  • Collaborate with solution architects and partner with lead architects to contribute to system design, reference architectures, and baseline implementations.
  • Contribute to platform modernization by simplifying, harmonizing, and optimizing systems to improve scalability and performance.
  • Validate solutions through proofs of concept and prototyping prior to full implementation.
  • Develop and optimize large-scale distributed data systems using modern frameworks (e.g., Spark, Hadoop, distributed processing technologies).
  • Collaborate with Data Science teams to implement advanced algorithms, models, and proprietary capabilities into production systems.
  • Ensure high availability, resilience, and performance of critical data processing systems (e.g., identity resolution/Match platforms).
  • Work cross-functionally with business stakeholders, QA, DevOps, and product teams to align technical solutions with business objectives.
  • Ensure code quality through testing, peer reviews, and adherence to development standards.
  • Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, automation, and modern engineering practices.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve complex production issues using strong root cause analysis skills.
  • Ensure compliance with data governance, information security, and regulatory standards.

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related areas
  • At least 5 years of experience developing commercial software
  • Strong proficiency in Java and Python with solid object-oriented design principles
  • Experience building large-scale distributed systems and data platforms
  • Expertise in big data frameworks (e.g., Hadoop, Spark, EMR, Dataproc)
  • Advanced SQL and data modeling knowledge
  • Experience developing cloud-native solutions (AWS, GCP, or equivalent)
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing frameworks, and monitoring tools
  • Knowledge of microservices architecture and high-availability systems
  • Strong problem-solving and root cause analysis capabilities
  • Ability to work independently while collaborating in cross-functional teams
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite skills
  • Show an ownership mindset in everything you do. Be a problem solver, be curious and be inspired to take action. Be proactive, seek ways to collaborate and connect with people and teams in support of driving success.
  • Continuous growth mindset, keep learning through social experiences and relationships with stakeholders, experts, colleagues and mentors as well as widen and broaden your competencies through structural courses and programs.
  • Where applicable, fluency in English and languages relevant to the working market.

lists.content:

• Good communication, interpersonal problem solving, and time management skills

• Ability to work in dynamic, results driven and fast pace culture
